summary: Scenario 2 Write-back Flow testing completed successfully. All 15 test steps verified - users can add new parts and save to master, modify existing part pricing and save brand-specific overrides.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted test part 'TEST Custom Part WriteBack' during testing. Restored AC Compressor to original state after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Write-back flow fully verified. saveRepairToMaster function works correctly for both new parts and existing part price overrides.

features_tested.step: 1. Open LiveProgressModal Repairs tab for an inspection
features_tested.evidence: Opened Singh - BR02BA4253 inspection, clicked Repairs tab (40 items)

features_tested.step: 2. Verify 'Manual Repair Entries' section shows vehicle info (brand, type)
features_tested.evidence: Manual Repair Entries section shows: Vehicle: MARUTI SUZUKI INDIA LTD | Type: Sedan | 31 parts in catalog

features_tested.step: 3. Click 'Add Repair Item' button to add a new manual entry
features_tested.evidence: Clicked 'Add Repair Item' button, new repair item row added with index #1

features_tested.step: 4. Enter a custom part name not in the master (e.g., 'Test Custom Part')
features_tested.evidence: Entered 'TEST Custom Part WriteBack' in Part/Item field

features_tested.step: 5. Enter Part Price and Labor values
features_tested.evidence: Entered Part Price: 5000, Labor: 1500, Total auto-calculated: ₹6,500

features_tested.step: 6. Verify 'Save to Master' button appears (green button) for unsaved entries
features_tested.evidence: Green 'Save to Master' button appeared next to delete button for new part entry

features_tested.step: 7. Verify 'Unsaved Changes' indicator appears at bottom
features_tested.evidence: 'Unsaved Changes' indicator visible at bottom of modal, 'Save All Changes' button highlighted with red asterisk

features_tested.step: 8. Click 'Save to Master' button to save new part to master
features_tested.evidence: Clicked 'Save to Master' button, API call successful

features_tested.step: 9. Verify success toast appears: 'New part added to master'
features_tested.evidence: Toast message: 'New part "TEST Custom Part WriteBack" added to master'

features_tested.step: 10. Select an existing part from catalog (click Package icon)
features_tested.evidence: Clicked Package icon, parts selector dropdown opened showing 31 parts including newly created test part. Selected 'AC Compressor'
Modified Part Price from 3500 to 4500, added Labor: 1000, Total: ₹5,500

features_tested.step: 12. Verify 'Custom pricing (not saved to master)' warning appears
features_tested.evidence: Orange warning '⚠️ Custom pricing (not saved to master)' appeared below Part # field

features_tested.step: 13. Click 'Save to Master' to update pricing in master
features_tested.evidence: Clicked 'Save to Master' button, API call successful

features_tested.step: 14. Verify success toast: 'Pricing for {part} saved to master'
features_tested.evidence: Toast message: 'Pricing for "AC Compressor" saved to master'

features_tested.step: 15. Verify parts catalog updates with new part/pricing
features_tested.evidence: Backend verification confirmed: 1) New part created with sedan pricing and brand override for MARUTI SUZUKI INDIA LTD. 2) AC Compressor updated with brand_overrides array containing MARUTI SUZUKI INDIA LTD pricing (repair_price: 4500, repair_labor: 1000)

POST /api/repair-parts (Create new part).response: 201 Created - Part created with id, sedan pricing, and brand_overrides

PUT /api/repair-parts/{id} (Update exist.response: 200 OK - Part updated with brand_overrides for MARUTI SUZUKI INDIA LTD

GET /api/repair-parts (Verify changes).response: 200 OK - Both new part and updated pricing verified in response

save_to_master_button.location: RepairItem component, next to delete button
save_to_master_button.visibility: Shows when hasUnsavedChanges is true (price_source is 'manual' or 'manual_override')

saveRepairToMaster_function_verified.new_part_flow: Creates new part with sedan/hatchback/suv pricing based on carType, adds brand_overrides if vehicle brand is available
saveRepairToMaster_function_verified.existing_part_flow: Updates brand_overrides array with new pricing for current vehicle brand, or updates default carType pricing if no brand

test_data_cleanup.ac_compressor_restored: Removed brand_overrides, restored to original state
